summary: Backend-only smoke and integration testing for the Household Expense Tracker. All 18 pytest tests passed (100%) against the public preview URL. Coverage includes auth (me/logout, invalid + expired tokens), household get + invite-code join (success + 404), categories & subcategories CRUD, default-category auto-seed (returns 8), expenses CRUD with base64 receipt round-trip and enriched fields (category_name, subcategory_name, paid_by_name), expenses filters (date range + category_id), dashboard aggregation across all 6 periods (daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly/biannual/yearly) with by_category (color/icon/subcategories) and by_earner, invalid period 400, and cross-household data isolation for both categories and expenses. Auth was tested by seeding user_sessions/users/households directly in Mongo (Google OAuth cannot be scripted), which is the sanctioned test path per PRD.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown'). Consider migrating to lifespan handlers (non-blocking; not a failure).
POST /api/auth/session does not return HTTP 201; also POST /api/categories & POST /api/expenses return 200 instead of 201. Minor REST hygiene, no functional impact.

dashboard._period_range uses date.replace(month=...) which will raise on Feb 29 in leap years for quarterly/biannual on day-of-month edges — currently safe because it uses day=1 or day=28-anchored math, but worth documenting.
server.py is 639 lines — approaching the 700-line threshold; consider splitting into auth/household/categories/expenses/dashboard routers.
No pagination on GET /api/expenses (hard-capped at 1000). Acceptable for MVP but should be paginated before scale.
seed_data_creation: Each test that needs auth uses per-test fixtures (seed_a, seed_b) that insert TEST-prefixed households/users/user_sessions and 8 default categories directly into MongoDB, then delete them + any created categories/expenses in teardown. No production/seed data is modified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_backend_api.py + conftest.py — they seed sessions directly and clean up after each test. To exercise a new endpoint, add a class to test_backend_api.py using the same seed_a/seed_b/auth_headers/client fixtures. Base URL comes from EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL in /app/frontend/.env; Mongo creds from /app/backend/.env. Frontend (Expo web) not tested this pass because Emergent Google OAuth cannot be scripted end-to-end.
rca_of_issue: No issues found. Backend implementation matches PRD and passes all functional + isolation checks.
